mcqs.question: What is a parameterized constructor?

options.text: A constructor that takes no parameters
A constructor that takes one or more parameters for initialization
A constructor that returns a value
A constructor that is called automatically

mcqs.correctAnswer: A constructor that takes one or more parameters for initialization
mcqs.explanation: Parameterized constructors accept arguments to initialize data members with specific values at object creation time.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is used to prevent implicit conversions using single-parameter constructors?
mcqs.correctAnswer: explicit
mcqs.explanation: The explicit keyword prevents the compiler from using single-parameter constructors for implicit type conversions.

mcqs.question: What is the correct syntax for calling a parameterized constructor with arguments?
mcqs.correctAnswer: ClassName obj(10,20);
mcqs.explanation: Parameters are passed in parentheses immediately after the object name during instantiation.

mcqs.question: What is constructor overloading?

options.text: Defining multiple constructors with same parameters
Defining multiple constructors with different parameters

mcqs.correctAnswer: Defining multiple constructors with different parameters
mcqs.explanation: Constructor overloading allows multiple constructors with different parameter lists to provide flexibility in object creation.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of initialization list in constructors?

options.text: To initialize members before constructor body executes

mcqs.correctAnswer: To initialize members before constructor body executes
mcqs.explanation: Initialization list allows direct initialization of members before the constructor body runs, which is essential for const and reference members.

mcqs.correctAnswer: Parameterized constructor with initialization list
mcqs.explanation: This is a parameterized constructor that accepts three integers and uses an initialization list to initialize the member variables.

mcqs.question: What is a copy constructor?

options.text: A constructor that copies data to a file
A constructor that takes another object as parameter
A constructor that returns a copy of the object

mcqs.correctAnswer: A constructor that takes another object as parameter
mcqs.explanation: A copy constructor takes a reference to another object of the same class and initializes a new object as its copy.

mcqs.question: What is the signature of the default copy constructor?
mcqs.correctAnswer: ClassName(const ClassName& obj)
mcqs.explanation: The copy constructor takes a const reference to avoid modification of the source object and enable passing by value.

mcqs.question: If we define a parameterized constructor, what happens to the default constructor?

options.text: It is deleted and not available

mcqs.correctAnswer: It is deleted and not available
mcqs.explanation: When any constructor is explicitly defined, the compiler no longer provides a default constructor.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a way to create an object using a parameterized constructor?
mcqs.correctAnswer: ClassName obj = 10;
mcqs.explanation: This syntax works only for single-parameter constructors with implicit conversion, but not as a general rule for all parameterized constructors.

mcqs.question: What is the output order in constructor execution?
mcqs.correctAnswer: Initialization list, then constructor body
mcqs.explanation: Members are initialized via the initialization list first, then the constructor body executes.

mcqs.question: What is the benefit of using 'explicit' keyword?
mcqs.correctAnswer: Prevents implicit conversions
mcqs.explanation: The explicit keyword prevents the compiler from using the constructor for unintended type conversions.

mcqs.question: Can a constructor have default arguments?

options.text: Yes, but only one default argument
Yes, multiple default arguments are allowed

mcqs.correctAnswer: Yes, multiple default arguments are allowed
mcqs.explanation: Parameterized constructors can have default argument values, making them callable with fewer arguments.

mcqs.question: What is delegated constructor?

options.text: Constructor calling another constructor in same class

mcqs.correctAnswer: Constructor calling another constructor in same class
mcqs.explanation: A delegated constructor uses another constructor of the same class to perform initialization.

mcqs.question: What is the correct order of member initialization in initialization list?

options.text: Order of declaration in class, not order in list

mcqs.correctAnswer: Order of declaration in class, not order in list
mcqs.explanation: Members are initialized in the order they are declared in the class, regardless of the order in the initialization list.

mcqs.question: Which constructor is called when: MyClass obj1; MyClass obj2 = obj1;

options.text: Parameterized constructor for obj1, copy constructor for obj2
Default for obj1, default for obj2

mcqs.correctAnswer: Parameterized constructor for obj1, copy constructor for obj2
mcqs.explanation: First statement calls default constructor, second statement uses copy constructor to initialize obj2 from obj1.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of default copy constructor?

options.text: O(n) where n is number of members

mcqs.correctAnswer: O(n) where n is number of members
mcqs.explanation: Copy constructor performs member-wise copy, so time complexity is linear in the number of data members.

mcqs.question: When should we use initialization list over assignment in constructor body?
mcqs.watch: When initializing const members, reference members, or base class constructors
mcqs.explanation: Initialization list is more efficient and required for const/references and should be used generally for better performance.
mcqs.correctAnswer: A

mcqs.question: What is the return type of a constructor?
mcqs.correctAnswer: No return type
mcqs.explanation: Constructors do not have a return type, not even void. They are special member functions.

mcqs.question: What happens if initialization list has uninitialized members?

options.text: They remain uninitialized with garbage values

mcqs.correctAnswer: They remain uninitialized with garbage values
mcqs.explanation: Uninitialized members in initialization list or not listed will contain indeterminate values unless they have default member initializers.
